description: Unified chart style registry for spatial-machines. Defines visual profiles per chart family, typography, palette routing, and default figure sizes. Mirrors config/map_styles.json conventions.

distribution.description: Show the shape of a single variable (histogram, KDE, box, violin). Pairs with choropleth maps to reveal skew and outliers.

comparison.description: Compare values across categories/geographies (bar, grouped bar, lollipop, dot plot). Default report figure alongside a ranked choropleth.

relationship.description: Bivariate / multivariate relationships (scatter, scatter+OLS, correlation heatmap, hexbin). Pairs with bivariate maps.

timeseries.description: Temporal change (line, area, small multiples over time). Used in change-detection narratives.

subtitle.family: Gill Sans MT, Gill Sans, Calibri, sans-serif

axis_label.family: Gill Sans MT, Gill Sans, Calibri, sans-serif

tick_label.family: Gill Sans MT, Gill Sans, Calibri, sans-serif

legend_title.family: Gill Sans MT, Gill Sans, Calibri, sans-serif

legend_label.family: Gill Sans MT, Gill Sans, Calibri, sans-serif

attribution.family: Gill Sans MT, Gill Sans, Calibri, sans-serif

annotation.family: Gill Sans MT, Gill Sans, Calibri, sans-serif

palettes_categorical._description: Categorical palettes — authoritative copy lives in config/palettes.json under the `categorical` key. This block is kept in sync for fast chart-side access; both files must list the same hex values.

_notes.palette_routing: Chart palettes resolve through config/map_styles.json -> palettes.sequential / diverging via field-name lookup in domain_palette_map, falling back to family default_palette. Categorical charts use palettes_categorical above.
_notes.colorblind: Run scripts/core/check_colorblind.py on any chart using color to encode category. Prefer colorblind_safe palette for >3 categories.
